code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a comics character (or related entities)
    into a single fluent sentence.  Predicates for the same subject are merged
    into one clause to produce a more natural, compact description.
    """
    # Mapping of predicate to a template string; placeholders are {subject} and {object}
    templates = {
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "creator": "{subject} was created by {object}",
        "fullName": "{subject}'s full name is {object}",
        "foundedBy": "{subject} was founded by {object}",
        "keyPerson": "{object} is a key person at {subject}",
        "city": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "product": "{subject} produces {object}",
        "lastAired": "{subject} last aired on {object}",
        "starring": "{subject} stars {object}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": "{subject} first appeared in the film {object}",
        "voice": "{subject} is voiced by {object}",
        "distributor": "The film {subject} was distributed by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} is {object}",
        "award": "{subject} received the {object}",
        "broadcastedBy": "{subject} was broadcasted by {object}",
        "firstAired": "{subject} first aired on {object}",
        "series": "{subject} is part of the series {object}",
        "child": "{subject} has a child named {object}",
    }

    # Group clauses by subject
    subject_groups: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        # Strip surrounding quotes from object if present
        obj = triple.object
        if obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subject=triple.subject, object=obj)
        else:
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {obj}"
        subject_groups.setdefault(triple.subject, []).append(clause)

    # Build merged clauses per subject
    merged_clauses: list[str] = []
    for subject, clauses in subject_groups.items():
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            merged_clauses.append(clauses[0])
        else:
            # Keep the first clause as‑is, strip the subject from subsequent ones
            first = clauses[0]
            rest = []
            for cl in clauses[1:]:
                # Remove leading "subject " (including possible trailing space)
                prefix = f"{subject} "
                if cl.startswith(prefix):
                    cl = cl[len(prefix):]
                rest.append(cl)
            # Join with commas and a final "and"
            if len(rest) == 1:
                combined = f"{first} and {rest[0]}"
            else:
                combined = f"{first}, " + ", ".join(rest[:-1]) + f", and {rest[-1]}"
            merged_clauses.append(combined)

    if not merged_clauses:
        return ""

    # Join different subject clauses with a semicolon for clarity
    sentence_body = "; ".join(merged_clauses)

    # Capitalise first character and ensure terminal period
    sentence = sentence_body[0].upper() + sentence_body[1:]
    if not sentence.endswith('.'):
        sentence += '.'
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
